content:
{
  "title": "FairPrompt: Adaptive Contrastive Prompting for Bias Mitigation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often reflect and amplify social biases and stereotypes present in their training data, even when given ostensibly neutral prompts. Current prompting techniques lack mechanisms to actively identify and counteract such biases during inference.",
  "motivation": "Mitigating social biases in LLMs is urgent as these models are increasingly deployed in high-stakes, real-world applications. While post-processing and fine-tuning approaches exist, prompt-based interventions offer a lightweight, model-agnostic alternative. However, most bias-reducing prompts are static and not tailored to the specific context of an input or the model's response tendencies.",
  "method": "We propose FairPrompt, an adaptive contrastive prompting technique that dynamically generates counterfactual prompts for each input by substituting demographic or identity variables and soliciting paired model outputs. During inference, the model is presented with both the original and a set of automatically generated counterfactual prompts (e.g., swapping gender, ethnicity, or age descriptors). The final response is selected or synthesized based on minimizing divergence between the outputs, encouraging fairness and reducing bias amplification. Optionally, a bias detection module can trigger this mechanism only when bias-prone content is detected.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate FairPrompt on standard bias benchmarks such as StereoSet, CrowS-Pairs, and WinoBias, comparing against vanilla prompting, instruction tuning, and manually crafted bias-mitigating prompts. Metrics will include bias scores, output consistency, and utility preservation. Human evaluation will further assess the perceived fairness and informativeness of the generated text."
}
